summary: Fire, Ship > Truckin', and soaring Phil bass dominate a high‑energy night
blurb: The second set erupts with a scorching Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain and a sweet Ship Of Fools that rolls straight into an epic Truckin'. Phil’s thumping bass shines on Knockin' On Heaven's Door, while the band closes strong with Heaven's Door and Attics Of My Life. Soundboard recordings capture the set in clear, balanced detail.
review: On March 28, 1993 at Albany’s Knickerbocker Arena the Dead delivered a punchy, high‑energy performance that still feels fresh. The first set built momentum with So Many Roads, High Time and a crisp Deal, but it was the second set where the magic unfolded – Scarlet Begonias segued into Fire On The Mountain with relentless drive, followed by a soulful Ship Of Fools that transitioned (albeit briefly rough) into a soaring Truckin'. After an inspired drum‑and‑space excursion the band rolled out Way To Go Home, then treated fans to Attics Of My Life and a blistering Turn On Your Lovelight. The encore’s Knockin' On Heaven's Door showcased Jerry’s vocal strength and Phil’s “on fire” bass that many reviewers called huge and thumping all night. Across all available tapes the show is praised for its solid playing, vibrant dynamics, and especially the post‑drum segment that was highlighted as very good. The top‑notch soundboard capture (gd1993-03-28.sbd.gans.miller.96215.sbeok.flac16) offers crystal‑clear balance with no dropouts, making it the definitive listening source.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All five recordings carry a positive sentiment; reviewers repeatedly use terms like 'top‑notch', 'high‑energy', 'smoking Scarlet > Fire', and praise Phil's bass as 'huge' and Jerry's vocals as solid. No consistent negative comments about performance were found, just minor recording flavor notes, so a strong positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Top‑notch soundboard source with clear, balanced audio and no dropouts; captures all highlighted songs in excellent fidelity.

key_highlights: Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain sequence ignites the second set
Ship Of Fools rolls into a standout Truckin' despite a brief rough transition
Phil Lesh’s bass is described as huge, thumping and on fire throughout
Post‑drum segment praised as very good; overall high‑energy performance

band_performance.Jerry: Solid guitar work and strong vocals, especially on Knockin' On Heaven's Door.
band_performance.Phil: Huge, thumping bass that dominates the night; highlighted as a major show highlight.
band_performance.Bob: Delivers a nice Lovelight moment during the set, adding vocal flair.
band_performance.Mickey: Consistently tight drumming, contributing to an excellent post‑drum segment.
band_performance.Billy: Solid rhythmic foundation throughout; part of the praised drum duo.
